Further, it was considering imposing tax surcharges (skattetillägg) amounting to SEK 147,000 (approximately 15,800 euros [EUR]), i.e. 40% of the increased tax liability on the sum of SEK 1,225,000.

The preliminary consideration was based on an investigation of a [ORG_ZJL] which had done business with a substantial number of [PER_GXD]. As concerned the [PER_VDW], it appeared that [PER_XKB] had borrowed SEK 40,000,000 from the [ORG_ZJL] and commissioned it to buy stocks for the same amount on his behalf. [PER_OGK] then sold the stocks back to the [ORG_ZJL] at a fixed date, upon which [PER_XKB] had made a capital gain. At the same time, [PER_XKB] repaid the loan together with interest to the [ORG_ZJL]. As the capital gain corresponded to the interest, they were set off against each other. In the [ORG_YEW]’s view, the transactions had in reality never taken place and the [PER_VDW] had simply received two statements of account and a promissory note in order to use them to obtain tax advantages.

[PER_OGK] was requested to submit any comments [PER_XKB] might have to the [ORG_YEW] by 22 [DATE_KOC], which [PER_XKB] did. [PER_XKB] claimed that the transactions had taken place and that his tax return should be approved.

By letter dated 6 [DATE_JBH] the [PER_VXE] informed the [ORG_KTC] that [PER_WJV] did not accept the [ORG_FXF]'s recommendation for transfer to open conditions, and that the next review should begin after 12 months. In the letter to the [PER_CFD] which accompanied the decision it was stated that: “... [t]he [ORG_NOC] attaches particular weight to the [PER_IGW]'s report, it has highlighted in some detail, the need for further exploration of your insight into, and responsibility for, the index offence and the apparent lack of empathy towards the [PER_PDK].”

The [ORG_VUG] stated that a copy of the decision letter was sent to the [PER_CFD] on 6 [DATE_JBH] and a copy to the [ORG_ZWB] then acting for the [PER_CFD] on 7 [DATE_XZT]. When the [PER_CFD] changed his [ORG_ZWB] in [DATE_SRH], the [ORG_OQA] sent a copy of the decision to the new [ORG_ZWB].

[PER_SWG] stated that [PER_WJV] wanted to seek judicial review of the decision, but was unable to find a [PER_FBT] who was prepared to act on his behalf. [PER_AWO] gave advice that judicial review stood little prospect of success as the reports recommending a move to open conditions had been slightly ambiguous, there were a number of very unfavourable reports and the [PER_VXE] had given extensive reasons for rejecting the recommended move.

The [PER_CFD]'s first [ORG_FXF] oral hearing under new arrangements was scheduled for [DATE_IIQ]. At the [PER_CFD]'s request, it was deferred until [DATE_LHQ] to allow the [PER_CFD] time to complete certain courses identified in the reports for the [ORG_FXF] review.

The case originated in an application ([CODE_EAO]) against the [ORG_DGI] lodged with the [ORG_XOH] (“the [ORG_VBZ]”) under former [CODE_LQG] of the Convention for the Protection of Human Rights and Fundamental Freedoms (“the Convention”) by a [PER_MMA], [PER_JKP] (“the [PER_UBV]”), on 28 [DATE_CYV].

[PER_HQF] was until 31 [DATE_YLO] represented by [PER_FZR]. [PER_TFX] and thereafter by [PER_IPP]. [PER_JQA], both lawyers practising in [LOC_JMT]. [ORG_UYJ] (“the [ORG_LSP]”) were represented by their [PER_MSM], [PER_YBY]. [PER_NFJ], [ORG_EZS].

[PER_HQF] alleged, in particular, that his rights under [CODE_GHT] of the Convention had been violated on account of the lack of an oral hearing before the [ORG_OVK] in proceedings concerning social security benefits.

The application was transmitted to the [ORG_CNV] on 1 [DATE_YXC], when [CODE_TPE] to the Convention came into force ([CODE_AOZ] § 2 of [CODE_TPE]).

The application was allocated to the [ORG_BEC] ([CODE_KUE] of the Rules of Court). Within that Section, the [ORG_OKL] that would consider the case ([CODE_EJF] § 1 of the Convention) was constituted as provided in [CODE_OAX].

By a decision of 4 [DATE_YLO] the [ORG_CNV] declared the application partly admissible.

On 1 [DATE_QRY] the [ORG_CNV] changed the composition of its Sections ([CODE_XYH]). This case was assigned to the newly composed [ORG_ZGA].

THE FACTS

I. THE CIRCUMSTANCES OF THE CASE

On 6 [DATE_JJR] the [PER_UBV], who was born in 1919, applied for disability benefits under the Social Insurance Act (Lagen om allmän försäkring, [IP_VPA]; hereinafter “the 1962 Act”). [PER_MEB] claimed that [PER_MEB] was in need of assistance and had additional costs due to a speech impediment caused by a laryngectomy operation in 1979.

By a decision of 24 [DATE_LIN] the [ORG_SFV] ([ORG_YUQ]; hereinafter “the Office”) of the [LOC_LHY] rejected the application, finding that the [PER_UBV]’s needs or costs were not such as to make him eligible for disability benefits.

On 27 [DATE_ZSI] the [PER_UBV] requested the [ORG_CZG] to review its decision under chapter 20, section 10 a of the 1962 Act. [PER_MEB] claimed that [PER_MEB] had failed to fully state his need of assistance and his additional costs in the original application. On 27 [DATE_FZU] [PER_MEB] asked the [ORG_CZG], alternatively, to consider his request as a new application and to grant him benefits as from [DATE_OFE].

On 12 [DATE_ILK] the [ORG_CZG] found that there was no basis for a review of its earlier decision. Further, in regard to the [PER_UBV]’s alternative claim, the [ORG_CZG] noted that the [PER_UBV] had reached the age of 65 in 1984 and that information relating to the time that followed could not be taken into account under the relevant rules. Consequently, the [PER_UBV]’s alternative claim was rejected.

[PER_HQF] appealed to the [ORG_PPS] ([ORG_GSS]) of the [LOC_LHY]. On 13 [DATE_HLQ] the [ORG_CNV] gave judgment in his favour. It considered that the [ORG_CZG]’s first decision had been based on insufficient information and that the [ORG_CZG] should therefore have reviewed that decision at the [PER_UBV]’s request. It noted that, according to the two medical certificates in the case, the [PER_UBV] had considerable additional costs due to his handicap. Consequently, [PER_MEB] was entitled to disability benefits as long as his need of assistance and his additional costs, taken together, attained the level required under the 1962 Act. Accepting the information submitted by the [PER_UBV] in this regard, the [ORG_CNV] found that that level had been attained and granted [PER_PIN] a disability allowance as from [DATE_XZZ]. The [ORG_CNV] did not hold an oral hearing nor, apparently, did the [PER_UBV] request one.

[ORG_ULF] ([ORG_NGU]; hereinafter “[ORG_RPD]”) appealed against the [ORG_PPS]’s judgment to the [ORG_OVK] ([ORG_IHD]) in [LOC_JMT], requesting that the [ORG_CZG]’s decision of 12 [DATE_ILK] be confirmed. The [ORG_HLB] claimed that the information in the case showed that, before the age of 65, the [PER_UBV] had not suffered a functional impairment to such an extent that his need of assistance and extra expenses on account thereof had made him eligible for a disability allowance. Thus, the [ORG_CZG]’s first decision could not be considered to have been incorrect due to it being based on incorrect or incomplete material. Consequently, there were no grounds for a review under the 1962 Act. [ORG_RPD] further requested the [ORG_SEB] to stay the enforcement of the appealed judgment. The latter request was granted by a decision of 30 [DATE_MZH].

[PER_HQF] made submissions in response to the [ORG_HLB]’s appeal. On 25 [DATE_OBA] [PER_MEB] requested the [ORG_OVK] to hold an oral hearing in the case during which the [PER_BEQ] in charge of the case at the [ORG_CZG] should give evidence relating to the meaning of its decision and the information on which it had been based. The [PER_UBV] also asked the [ORG_CNV] to obtain the opinion of a medical expert.

On 12 [DATE_ZTP] the [ORG_OVK] rejected both requests. In regard to the request for an oral hearing, the [ORG_CNV], after having restated section 9 of the Administrative [ORG_CNV] Procedure Act (Förvaltningsprocesslagen, [IP_TQE]; hereinafter “the 1971 Act”; see further paragraph 23 below), gave the following reasons: “Having regard to the subject-matter at issue and the information that has come to hand in the case, [the [ORG_CNV]] finds that an oral hearing is unnecessary and rejects the request to that effect. [[PER_HQF]] is invited to state the further circumstances [PER_MEB] wishes to invoke and submit his final written observations in the case within two weeks after having been notified of this decision. The case can be determined notwithstanding a failure to submit such written observations.”

[PER_HQF] and the [ORG_HLB] made further submissions. The [PER_UBV] maintained that the [ORG_HLB] had failed to adequately explain the reasons for its appeal and posed several questions to the [ORG_HLB]. [PER_MEB] requested that the [ORG_CNV] order the [ORG_HLB] to clarify its position in the case. The [ORG_CNV] did not do so, however. The [PER_UBV]’s submissions were, however, forwarded to the [ORG_HLB] which replied that it maintained its position in the case. On account of the [ORG_CNV]’s refusal to order the [ORG_HLB] to clarify its position and to hold a hearing, the [PER_UBV] called into question the impartiality of the proceedings. By letters of 16 [DATE_VKL] and 10 [DATE_PFZ] [PER_MEB] reiterated his request for an oral hearing, stating that a hearing would give [PER_PIN] the opportunity to ask questions to the [ORG_HLB] and to present judgments in similar cases in support of his application for disability benefits. Furthermore, in assessing his condition, the [ORG_CNV] would benefit from having met [PER_PIN] in person.

On 1 [DATE_FGJ] the [ORG_OVK] rejected the [PER_UBV]’s renewed requests for an oral hearing and gave judgment in favour of the [ORG_HLB]. Thus, by 3 votes to 2, it quashed the [ORG_PPS]’s judgment and confirmed the [ORG_CZG]’s decision of 12 [DATE_ILK]. Without giving any further reasons, it considered that there had been no basis for a review of the [ORG_CZG]’s first decision. The dissenting judges considered that the [PER_UBV] was entitled to the disability allowance granted to him by the [ORG_PPS] and that, for this reason, there were exceptional reasons to change the [ORG_CZG]’s decision.

[PER_HQF] appealed to the [ORG_ZCU] ([ORG_GLX]). [PER_MEB] requested that the case be referred back to the [ORG_OVK] for re-examination or, alternatively, that the [ORG_ZCU] confirm the [ORG_PPS]’s judgment. [PER_MEB] complained about the lack of an oral hearing in the [ORG_OVK] and also requested the [ORG_ZCU] to hold an oral hearing. On 24 [DATE_CYV] the [ORG_ZCU] refused the [PER_UBV] leave to appeal.

On 9 [DATE_KZA] the [PER_UBV] made a renewed request for a disability allowance. By a decision of 12 [DATE_NNC] the [ORG_CZG] found that [PER_MEB] had special needs and costs due to his handicap which made [PER_PIN] eligible for disability benefits under the 1962 Act. As an allowance could be given retroactively only for the two years preceding the application, the [PER_UBV] was granted an allowance as from [DATE_ILK].

[PER_YCY] claimed that [PER_YCY] had failed to fully state his need of assistance and his additional costs in the original application. On 27 [DATE_CNI] [PER_YCY] asked the [ORG_ITM], alternatively, to consider his request as a new application and to grant him benefits as from [DATE_PFI].

By letters of 16 [DATE_AIQ] and 10 [DATE_AQA] [PER_YCY] reiterated his request for an oral hearing, stating that a hearing would give [PER_DYM] the opportunity to ask questions to the [ORG_LEO] and to present judgments in similar cases in support of his application for disability benefits. [PER_YCY] requested that the case be referred back to the [ORG_YVB] for re-examination or, alternatively, that the [ORG_YQB] confirm the [ORG_QOU]’s judgment. [PER_YCY] complained about the lack of an oral hearing in the [ORG_YVB] and also requested the [ORG_YQB] to hold an oral hearing. On 9 [DATE_EKY] the [PER_FGH] made a renewed request for a disability allowance. By a decision of 12 [DATE_QIZ] the [ORG_ITM] found that [PER_YCY] had special needs and costs due to his handicap which made [PER_DYM] eligible for disability benefits under the 1962 Act.
